title: Find and Sort — Algorithms
description: Add a search function that takes the names array and a search term, then returns the index of the matching product (or -1 if not found). Add a sort function that sorts all three parallel arrays by price using bubble sort or selection sort. Both must be standalone functions (not inside main). Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'searchByName' in cpp or 'search_by_name' in cpp or 'findProduct' in cpp or 'find_product' in cpp, 'Write a search function (e.g., searchByName)'
assert 'sortByPrice' in cpp or 'sort_by_price' in cpp or 'sortProducts' in cpp or 'sort_products' in cpp, 'Write a sort function (e.g., sortByPrice)'
assert cpp.count('int ') >= 3 or cpp.count('void ') >= 1, 'Define functions with proper return types'
assert '-1' in cpp, 'Search function should return -1 when product is not found'
assert 'swap' in cpp.lower() or 'temp' in cpp.lower(), 'Sort algorithm needs to swap elements (use a temp variable or swap())'
lines = cpp.split('\n')
func_outside_main = False
for line in lines:
    stripped = line.strip()
    if ('searchByName' in stripped or 'sortByPrice' in stripped or 'findProduct' in stripped or 'sortProducts' in stripped) and (stripped.startswith('int ') or stripped.startswith('void ')):
        func_outside_main = True
        break
assert func_outside_main, 'Search and sort functions must be defined outside of main (as standalone functions)'
acceptance_criteria: Search function defined outside main, takes array and target, returns index or -1
Sort function defined outside main, sorts all three parallel arrays by price
Sort uses bubble sort or selection sort with element swapping
Menu option 3 calls the search function and displays the result
Menu option 4 calls the sort function
hints: Linear search: loop through names[], compare each to the target, return i if found
Bubble sort: nested loops, swap adjacent elements if prices[j] > prices[j+1]
When sorting, swap elements in ALL three arrays together to keep data aligned
Pass arrays to functions by name (arrays decay to pointers automatically in C++)
